what_it_does: A generated CSS file that declares a single :root block full of custom-property design tokens (colors, spacing, radius, typography, shadows, layout, and device dimensions) so a host surface can resolve DNA/phone.css token references without importing DNA's global reset or body rules.
observations: Only a :root selector is present; no element/global reset or body rules.
The file is described as generated by scripts/sync-gallery.mjs from piece.css.
Contains a warm, neutral surface palette plus identity colors (gold, ochre, bronze, charcoal, etc.).
Defines a large enumerated spacing scale based on an 8px unit and a 1.5 ratio.
Includes radius, type, shadow, layout-margin, and device-dimension tokens.
Comments state the file is host-safe and that DNA owns the source tokens.
standouts: Generated extraction limited to :root tokens to avoid host style leakage.
Very long hand-enumerated token list rather than computed values.
Includes explicit device viewport size tokens (mobile, tablet, desktop).
conventions: CSS custom properties with semantic, prefixed names (--dna-*).
Generated artifact with a header comment explaining origin and constraints.
Warm, neutral color naming and systematic 8px-based spacing scale.
notes: This is a host-safe, generated :root token vocabulary extracted from DNA's piece.css so a surface can consume DNA/phone.css tokens without inheriting global resets.
questions: Why are spacing/type/radius values enumerated individually instead of generated with calc()?
How does scripts/sync-gallery.mjs decide which :root blocks to extract and how is it kept in sync?
Is this file meant to be edited manually or regenerated automatically?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments say it exposes only :root tokens from piece.css and excludes global reset/body rules, and the content is exactly a :root token block.
intention_signals: To create a portable, non-leaky token layer that lets DNA visual styles resolve inside a different host surface while preserving that surface's own global typography and reset.
novelty: The deliberate extraction of only :root custom properties from a source stylesheet to make a design-system token file safe for a host that already has its own global styles.
contribution: Provides a self-contained, importable :root token vocabulary that a fused system can keep to resolve DNA component styles in host surfaces without pulling in DNA's global reset or body rules.
summary_for_embedding: A generated CSS file containing DNA design tokens—colors, spacing, radius, typography, shadows, layout margin, and device dimensions—under a single :root selector for host-safe consumption.
intention_for_embedding: To expose DNA's token vocabulary to a host surface while excluding global reset and body rules that would alter the host.
